schemaVersion: Ehel English Course Final Assessment v1.1
subject: English
title: Grade 3 English Final Course Quiz

questions.quizTitle: All About Me and My Family checkpoint
questions.question: What does 'family' mean?
questions.options: A person who learns in school. | A person you like and spend time with. | A younger person, or someone in a lower class. | The people who live with you and love you.
questions.correctAnswer: The people who live with you and love you.
questions.explanation: My family has my mum, my dad, my sister, and me.
questions.sourceUnitTitle: All About Me and My Family

questions.quizTitle: All About Me and My Family checkpoint
questions.question: What does 'parent' mean?
questions.options: A mother or a father. | A person who learns in school. | A younger person, or someone in a lower class. | An older person, or someone who has learned more.
questions.correctAnswer: A mother or a father.
questions.explanation: My parents take me to school every morning.
questions.sourceUnitTitle: All About Me and My Family

questions.quizTitle: All About Me and My Family checkpoint
questions.question: What does 'friend' mean?
questions.options: A person you like and spend time with. | A younger person, or someone in a lower class. | An older person, or someone who has learned more. | To act in a good and kind way.
questions.correctAnswer: A person you like and spend time with.
questions.explanation: My friend Nora and I play football after school.
questions.sourceUnitTitle: All About Me and My Family

questions.quizTitle: All About Me and My Family checkpoint
questions.question: What does 'student' mean?
questions.options: A person who learns in school. | An older person, or someone who has learned more. | The way someone acts. | To act in a good and kind way.
questions.correctAnswer: A person who learns in school.
questions.explanation: Every student in my class can read now.
questions.sourceUnitTitle: All About Me and My Family

questions.question: What does 'teacher' mean?
questions.options: A person who helps us learn new things. | A place where you can find and borrow books. | The subject where we work with numbers, shapes, and measuring. | To finish school or a class.
questions.correctAnswer: A person who helps us learn new things.
questions.explanation: My teacher helps me with my mathematics homework.

questions.question: What does 'author' mean?
questions.options: A person who writes books or stories. | The rules for how we put words together to speak and write. | The subject where we work with numbers, shapes, and measuring. | To finish school or a class.
questions.correctAnswer: A person who writes books or stories.
questions.explanation: The author wrote a funny book about a talking camel.

questions.question: What does 'library' mean?
questions.options: A place where you can find and borrow books. | A time when we learn something new in school. | The rules for how we put words together to speak and write. | The subject where we work with numbers, shapes, and measuring.
questions.correctAnswer: A place where you can find and borrow books.
questions.explanation: I go to the library to read and borrow books.

questions.question: What does 'graduate' mean?
questions.options: A time when we learn something new in school. | Learning about things at school or at home. | The rules for how we put words together to speak and write. | To finish school or a class.
questions.correctAnswer: To finish school or a class.
questions.explanation: My sister will graduate from primary school next year.

questions.question: What does 'January' mean?
questions.options: The fifth month of the year. | The first month of the year. | The fourth month of the year, when flowers bloom in many places. | The third month of the year.
questions.correctAnswer: The first month of the year.
questions.explanation: The school year begins in January.

questions.question: What does 'February' mean?
questions.options: The fifth month of the year. | The fourth month of the year, when flowers bloom in many places. | The second month of the year, usually with 28 days. | The sixth month of the year, often warm and sunny.
questions.correctAnswer: The second month of the year, usually with 28 days.
questions.explanation: February is the shortest month.

questions.question: What does 'March' mean?
questions.options: The fifth month of the year. | The seventh month of the year, often very warm. | The sixth month of the year, often warm and sunny. | The third month of the year.
questions.correctAnswer: The third month of the year.
questions.explanation: The rains often come in March.

questions.question: What does 'April' mean?
questions.options: The eighth month of the year. | The fourth month of the year, when flowers bloom in many places. | The seventh month of the year, often very warm. | The sixth month of the year, often warm and sunny.
questions.correctAnswer: The fourth month of the year, when flowers bloom in many places.
questions.explanation: We plant our garden in April.

questions.question: What does 'doctor' mean?
questions.options: A person who helps sick people feel better. | A person who works on a boat or ship at sea. | A place where doctors and nurses care for people who are sick or hurt. | A place where people solve problems fairly using the laws.
questions.correctAnswer: A person who helps sick people feel better.
questions.explanation: The doctor works at the hospital.

questions.question: What does 'officer' mean?
questions.options: A person who helps keep people safe and makes sure rules are followed. | A place where doctors and nurses care for people who are sick or hurt. | A place where people solve problems fairly using the laws. | A school for older students and adults.
questions.correctAnswer: A person who helps keep people safe and makes sure rules are followed.
questions.explanation: The officer helped us cross the road.

questions.question: What does 'sailor' mean?
questions.options: A person who works on a boat or ship at sea. | A place where people buy and sell food and other things. | A place where people solve problems fairly using the laws. | A school for older students and adults.
questions.correctAnswer: A person who works on a boat or ship at sea.
questions.explanation: The sailor sails across the sea.

questions.question: What does 'hospital' mean?
questions.options: A place where doctors and nurses care for people who are sick or hurt. | A place where people buy and sell food and other things. | A place where plants, flowers or vegetables grow. | A school for older students and adults.
questions.correctAnswer: A place where doctors and nurses care for people who are sick or hurt.
questions.explanation: My sister is resting at the hospital.

questions.question: What does 'discuss' mean?
questions.options: To finish something or make it whole. | To make something by putting parts together. | To say kindly that you will give or do something. | To talk about something with other people.
questions.correctAnswer: To talk about something with other people.
questions.explanation: We will discuss our ideas in a group.

questions.question: What does 'listen' mean?
questions.options: To finish something or make it whole. | To make something by putting parts together. | To take something away from a place. | To use your ears to hear carefully.
questions.correctAnswer: To use your ears to hear carefully.
questions.explanation: Please listen to the teacher when she talks.

questions.question: What does 'offer' mean?
questions.options: To finish something or make it whole. | To grow or become better over time. | To say kindly that you will give or do something. | To take something away from a place.
questions.correctAnswer: To say kindly that you will give or do something.
questions.explanation: I will offer help if my friend falls down.

questions.question: What does 'build' mean?
questions.options: To grow or become better over time. | To keep someone or something safe from harm. | To make something by putting parts together. | To take something away from a place.
questions.correctAnswer: To make something by putting parts together.
questions.explanation: We will build a wall with smooth stones.

questions.question: What does 'kind' mean?
questions.options: Always tells the truth. | Nice and helpful to other people. | The act of being kind and helpful. | To help or worry about someone because you want them to be well.
questions.correctAnswer: Nice and helpful to other people.
questions.explanation: The kind girl shared her lunch with a new pupil.

questions.question: What does 'friendly' mean?
questions.options: Doing many things and having little free time. | Happy to talk to people and play with them. | The act of being kind and helpful. | To help or worry about someone because you want them to be well.
questions.correctAnswer: Happy to talk to people and play with them.
questions.explanation: Our new neighbour is very friendly.

questions.question: What does 'honest' mean?
questions.options: Always tells the truth. | Doing many things and having little free time. | Quiet and gentle; not angry or excited. | To help or worry about someone because you want them to be well.
questions.correctAnswer: Always tells the truth.
questions.explanation: Amal is honest, so she told the teacher what really happened.

questions.question: What does 'kindness' mean?
questions.options: Can do something. | Doing many things and having little free time. | Quiet and gentle; not angry or excited. | The act of being kind and helpful.
questions.correctAnswer: The act of being kind and helpful.
questions.explanation: She showed kindness by helping the boy who fell.

questions.question: What does 'climate' mean?
questions.options: How hot or cold something is. | The light and warmth that come from the sun. | The usual weather in a place over a long time. | Turned to ice because it was very cold. (past of freeze)
questions.correctAnswer: The usual weather in a place over a long time.
questions.explanation: The desert has a hot, dry climate.

questions.question: What does 'weather' mean?
questions.options: A very tall hill with steep sides. | The light and warmth that come from the sun. | Turned to ice because it was very cold. (past of freeze) | What the sky and air are like today.
questions.correctAnswer: What the sky and air are like today.
questions.explanation: Today the weather is sunny and warm.

questions.question: What does 'temperature' mean?
questions.options: A big area with many trees. | A very tall hill with steep sides. | How hot or cold something is. | Turned to ice because it was very cold. (past of freeze)
questions.correctAnswer: How hot or cold something is.
questions.explanation: The temperature is 30 degrees today.

questions.question: What does 'sunshine' mean?
questions.options: A big area with many trees. | A very tall hill with steep sides. | Land with sand near the sea. | The light and warmth that come from the sun.
questions.correctAnswer: The light and warmth that come from the sun.
questions.explanation: The sunshine helped the flowers grow.

questions.question: What does 'number' mean?
questions.options: A design or sequence that repeats. | A word or symbol that shows how many things there are. | Not bent or curved; in a line. | Something that is true and can be proven.
questions.correctAnswer: A word or symbol that shows how many things there are.
questions.explanation: My favourite number is seven.

questions.question: What does 'million' mean?
questions.options: A design or sequence that repeats. | A very big number: 1,000,000, which is one thousand thousands. | Not bent or curved; in a line. | Putting numbers together to find a total.
questions.correctAnswer: A very big number: 1,000,000, which is one thousand thousands.
questions.explanation: There are more than a million stars in the sky.
